info.title: AIPOM Orchestrator API
info.description: RESTful API for the AIPOM AI project orchestrator: the 9-phase plan lifecycle (discovery, architecture, enriching, the two human gates, execution, the replan loop), reference-data catalogs, two-tier config, and the live SSE event stream.

post.description: Human approval at the pre-execution gate: AWAITING_REVIEW -> RUNNING.
Human "finish" at the post-execution gate: REVIEW -> DONE.
Human "replan next phase" at the post-execution gate: REVIEW -> REPLANNING.
Chat-triggered mid-RUNNING replan: skip pending work -> REPLANNING.

One DISCOVERY conversation turn. Multi-turn: committed=false keeps the
conversation open; committed=true is the roadmap commit -> ARCHITECTURE.

One REPLANNING conversation turn. committed=true commits the new goal
set -> ARCHITECTURE (the iteration increments here).

get.description:
The plan's DISCOVERY/REPLANNING conversation, in order. 404s for an
unknown plan (chat rows only exist for real plans).

put.description:
Granular agent editing (#18): the repo-level guard against editing an
agent bound to a RUNNING task lands with the roadmap 3.5 mutation guards.
Rename only — a model's provider binding is immutable.

AgentSpec.title: AgentSpec
AgentSpec.description:
Definition of an agent: who it is, what it can do, how it retries,
and which runtime executes it.

`role` is the agent's functional job in the orchestration (e.g. "test_writer",
"implementer", "reviewer"). `model_role` is an indirection key into the
provider/model catalog naming a model *tier* (e.g. "cheap", "smart",
"long_context") resolved to a concrete IAModel at runtime — so swapping the
model behind a tier does not require touching every agent that uses it.

Runtime resolution (deliberate Phase-0 un-freeze, 2026-07-05): the agent
registry is the authority on which runtime an agent resolves to —
`runtime_type` picks the CLI runtime (or the dry-run dummy), and
`provider_id`/`model_id` point into the providers/models catalog for the
credentials and model string. Infra validates the wiring; the entity only
carries it.

Capability.title: Capability
Capability.description: A named capability an agent can satisfy, bundling the tools it implies.

EditRequest.title: EditRequest
EditRequest.description:
One structural edit. `type` selects the operation; the other fields are
per-type (validated in _to_edit so a bad combination 422s, not 500s).

FailureKind.title: FailureKind
FailureKind.description:
Typed classification of a task failure. Produced by the agent runner,
consumed by RetryPolicy.should_retry — a checked classification, not string
matching (DESIGN_NOTES #2).

MessageResponse.title: MessageResponse
MessageResponse.description:
One conversation turn: the assistant reply, whether the roadmap was
committed, and the (possibly advanced) phase.

RetryPolicy.title: RetryPolicy
RetryPolicy.description:
Domain rule for retry/terminal decisions.

Owns the *decision* (should we retry? how long to back off?); the adapter
owns the *mechanism* (actually sleeping, re-invoking). Persisted per-plan so
it is configurable and survives crashes. This is also what makes
"retries exhausted -> terminal FAILED" a domain rule rather than adapter magic.
